entries.summary: John Lee was sworn in as Hong Kong's chief executive on the handover anniversary.
Hong Kong's top court began hearing Jimmy Lai's appeal over national-security legal-team representation.
Lai was sentenced to five years and nine months over fraud charges in Hong Kong.
The trial of 47 pro-democracy activists began under the national security law.
Lai's national-security trial began at West Kowloon court after multiple delays.
Hong Kong unveiled draft Article 23 legislation to expand the national security law.
Hong Kong's legislature unanimously passed the Article 23 Safeguarding National Security Ordinance.
The Safeguarding National Security Ordinance entered into force across Hong Kong.
Hong Kong court convicted 14 pro-democracy activists in the Hong Kong 47 case.
A Hong Kong court convicted two Stand News editors of conspiracy to publish seditious material.
A Stand News editor received a 21-month jail term in the sedition case.
Hong Kong court sentenced 45 of the Hong Kong 47 to up to 10 years in jail.
Jimmy Lai testified for the first time in his own national-security trial.
Hong Kong police placed bounties on six overseas activists for alleged national-security offences.
Hong Kong revoked passports of seven activists living abroad under Article 23 powers.
Xi Jinping attended John Lee's swearing-in as Hong Kong's chief executive.
Apple Daily founder Jimmy Lai was jailed nearly six years on fraud charges.
Trial of 47 democrats began in what observers called Hong Kong's biggest national-security case.
Jimmy Lai's national security trial opened in Hong Kong's West Kowloon court.
Hong Kong unveiled new local national-security legislation to bolster Beijing's 2020 law.
Hong Kong's Legislative Council passed Article 23 national-security legislation in 12 days.
Hong Kong's Article 23 national-security law took effect across the territory.
A Hong Kong court convicted 14 democracy activists in the Hong Kong 47 trial.
Two former Stand News editors were found guilty of sedition after lengthy trial.
A former Stand News editor was sentenced to 21 months for sedition charges.
A Hong Kong court jailed 45 democracy activists for up to 10 years each.
Jimmy Lai took the witness stand for the first time in his trial.
Hong Kong activist Joshua Wong was sentenced to four years and eight months in prison.
Hong Kong put bounties on six democracy activists living abroad.
A Hong Kong court ruled the protest anthem Glory to Hong Kong should be banned.
